mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 6% for three consecutive months signals a weakening labor market that reduces corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising unemployment are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts below 800,000 units for three consecutive months signals a weakening demand for residential construction that reduces aggregate economic activity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with declining housing starts are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in industrial production above 110 for three consecutive months signals a strengthening supply chain that increases corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with rising industrial production are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate below 2% for three consecutive months signals a tightening monetary cycle that increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with declining federal funds rate are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with rising unemployment are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods with declining housing starts are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with rising industrial production are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
